description: Fix analytics dashboard stuck in loading/skeleton state. The root causes are: (1) the server-side aggregateMetrics function makes ~196 sequential Redis calls using type-cast operations (bitCount, pfCount) that may not exist or may hang on the Devvit Redis client, causing the HTTP request to never complete; (2) the client-side fetchMetrics has no timeout, so if the server hangs, the loading state persists indefinitely; (3) the AdminDashboard has poor visual feedback for error/empty states, making it appear stuck in a skeleton-like state even when errors occur.
steps: Step 1: Fix the server-side aggregateMetrics in src/server/lib/viral-analytics.ts. Wrap the type-cast Redis operations (bitCount, pfCount) in try-catch blocks with fallback to 0 so that missing/unsupported Redis commands don't crash the entire aggregation. Specifically: in getDailyMetrics(), wrap the Promise.all call in a try-catch that returns safe defaults (0 for all counters, empty funnel). In getRetentionRate(), wrap the bitCount calls in try-catch returning 0.

Step 2: Parallelize the aggregateMetrics function. Instead of a sequential for-loop calling getDailyMetrics one at a time (14 sequential iterations with 14+ Redis calls each), use Promise.all to fetch all 14 days in parallel. This reduces the total time from O(14 * latency_per_day) to O(latency_per_day).

Step 3: Fix the client-side fetchMetrics in src/client/stores/metrics.ts. Add a timeout (e.g., 15 seconds) using AbortController so that if the server never responds, the fetch is aborted and the loading state is cleared with an appropriate error message. This prevents the loading spinner from showing indefinitely.

Step 4: Improve the AdminDashboard.svelte UI states. Currently, the loading spinner shows alongside KPI cards that display '---' for null metrics. Update the template so that: (a) while loading, show a more prominent loading state (skeleton-style cards or a full-section loading indicator); (b) when an error occurs, show a clear error message with a retry button; (c) when data is loaded but all values are zero (no data yet), show a friendly 'No data yet' state instead of all-zero cards.
acceptance_criteria: The fetchMetrics function has a timeout mechanism (AbortController with ~15s timeout) that prevents indefinite loading
The aggregateMetrics function handles missing/unsupported Redis operations gracefully by catching errors and returning safe defaults (0 values)
The aggregateMetrics function uses Promise.all to parallelize daily metrics fetches instead of sequential loop
The AdminDashboard shows a clear error state with retry button when metrics fail to load
The AdminDashboard shows a proper empty state when no data is available
The project builds without errors (pnpm build passes)
Existing tests continue to pass (pnpm test passes)
verification: Run pnpm install && pnpm build to confirm no build/type errors
Run pnpm test to confirm existing tests pass
Verify the fetchMetrics function in src/client/stores/metrics.ts uses AbortController with a timeout
Verify getDailyMetrics in src/server/lib/viral-analytics.ts wraps Redis type-cast calls in try-catch
Verify aggregateMetrics uses Promise.all instead of sequential for-loop
